title: rosa canina var. segobricensis
intro: Rosa canina var. segobricensis, also known as the Alhucema or the Spanish rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, a flowering shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Rosa canina var. segobricensis has been traditionally used to promote digestive health, relieve menstrual cramps, and support the immune system, among other health benefits. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and infusions in oil, which can be used topically or ingested as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
